summaries.title: What’s new for developers (July 2026)
summaries.summary: WordPress 7.1 is set to release on August 19, 2026, with betas starting July 15. Key changes include responsive styling improvements, new blocks like Playlist and Tabs, and a shift to moderate speculative loading. Developers should test Gutenberg 23.5 and newer, as it requires WordPress 6.9 or higher. The responsive styling update deprecates useResizeCanvas() and introduces viewport-based style controls, affecting custom block and theme development. A new Abilities API proposal adds core read-only abilities like core/read-settings, while a Guidelines merge proposal introduces a wp_knowledge post type for centralized site knowledge management.

summaries.title: Transitioning To Rulesets In The Gutenberg Repository
summaries.summary: As of 16:00 UTC on July 8, 2026, the Gutenberg repository has transitioned from branch protection rules to repository rulesets to fix a broken step in its plugin release process. Rulesets offer more flexibility, layering capabilities, and public transparency compared to branch protection rules, with efforts made to ensure they function identically to previous protections.

summaries.title: Dev Chat Agenda – July 8, 2026
summaries.summary: The July 8, 2026 WordPress Developers Chat will focus on upcoming release discussions and include an open floor for general topics. The 7.1 beta is scheduled for July 15, with dev notes due by August 3 and a Field Guide targeted for August 4. The classic block will remain visible in the inserter in WP 7.1, and there are merge proposals for design system theming and Gutenberg 23.5 updates.

summaries.title: Merge Proposal: Design System Theming
summaries.summary: The Gutenberg Components Team proposes merging a theming system for WordPress admin UI, introducing design tokens and a ThemeProvider React component to enable consistent styling through CSS custom properties. A key technical takeaway is the inclusion of a tool for generating accessible color ramps from seed colors, enhancing customization and accessibility.

summaries.title: The Classic block stays in the inserter for WordPress 7.1
summaries.summary: The Classic block will remain in the inserter for WordPress 7.1, with no changes to user or developer behavior and no migration required. The decision was reversed due to feedback indicating the change made the experience worse without clear benefits, and efforts will now focus on improving alternatives to Classic blocks and migration tools.

summaries.title: WordPress 7.0.1 Maintenance Release
summaries.summary: WordPress 7.0.1 is a maintenance release addressing 31 bugs in Core and the Block Editor, with fixes impacting the block editor, admin UI, and media. The next major version, WordPress 7.1, is scheduled for release on August 19, 2026, at WordCamp US.

summaries.title: Roadmap 7.1, Gutenberg 23.5, Responsive Styling, Migration to Block themes — Weekend Edition #368
summaries.summary: WordPress 7.1 is scheduled for August 19, 2026, with key features including responsive and interactive-state styling, new blocks like Playlist and Tabs, and a redesigned command palette. Gutenberg 23.5 introduces responsive editing with drag-to-resize, magnified crop canvas in the media editor, and text shadows in Global Styles. Testing opportunities for responsive styling are now available.

summaries.title: WordPress 7.0.1 Fixes Registration Spam, wp_kses() CSS Corruption, and 7.0 Admin Design Glitches
summaries.summary: WordPress 7.0.1 addresses several critical issues from the 7.0 release, including stopping registration spam, fixing CSS corruption in `wp_kses()`, and resolving admin UI glitches. Key developer fixes include restoring the ability to dequeue `global-styles-inline-css`, ensuring PHP 8.5 compatibility, and reintroducing a deprecated Navigation function as a shim for migration.
